items.q: What is the main advantage of asymmetric encryption?
items.correct: Secure key exchange without sharing a secret in advance.
items.misconception: It is faster than symmetric encryption.
items.why: Students think asymmetric is just 'better'. It's actually SLOWER than symmetric. Its advantage is solving key distribution - the public key can be shared openly without compromising security.

items.q: What does an INNER JOIN return?
items.correct: Only rows with matching values in both tables.
items.misconception: All rows from both tables combined.
items.random_wrong: Only the inner columns of each table.
items.why: Students confuse INNER (intersection) with FULL OUTER (union). INNER drops unmatched rows from BOTH sides; LEFT keeps all from left; RIGHT keeps all from right; FULL OUTER keeps everything.

items.q: What happens when 11111111 + 1 is computed in an 8-bit register?
items.correct: Overflow - the result requires 9 bits but only 8 are available.
items.why: Students miss that registers have fixed width. The mathematical result is 9 bits, but the 8-bit register drops the carry-out, producing 00000000 with an overflow flag.

items.q: Which is a high-level programming language?
items.why: It's Python. “Assembly” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Integrated Development Environment. “Internet Data Engine” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In Python, how many spaces are conventionally used for one level of indentation (PEP 8)?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 spaces. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
